title: Persona username not validated, allowing path traversal in URLs
description: persona.rs add() accepts any string as a username and stores it in the DB. The server constructs URLs like 'https://{domain}/users/{username}' and filesystem paths like 'media/{id}.png' using the username. A username containing '/' or '..' would produce malformed ActivityPub URIs (e.g., https://example.com/users/../admin) and could confuse remote servers' routing. While the CLI is operator-controlled, there is no validation that the username matches [a-zA-Z0-9_]+ or similar safe pattern. A contributor adding a web-based persona creation endpoint would inherit this gap. Fix: validate username against a strict alphanumeric+underscore pattern in persona::add().
